channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Warsh to Reiterate Fed’s Pledge to Get Inflation Down
item.description: Kevin M. Warsh, since becoming chairman of the Federal Reserve, has declined to say whether he supports higher interest rates to tame price pressures.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kevin Warsh, chairman of the Federal Reserve, at the Marriner S. Eccles Federal Reserve Board Building in Washington, D.C., in June.

item.title: Big Banks Smash Earnings Records, but ‘Tectonic’ Risks Loom
item.description: The largest banks in the United States collectively raked in tens of billions of profits in the second quarter, despite the war in Iran and persistent inflation.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Stacy Cowley
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The headquarters of JPMorgan Chase in Manhattan. The bank and three of its largest rivals reported a collective $43 billion in second-quarter profits,

item.title: U.S. Workers Are More Productive Than Ever. A.I. Isn’t the Key.
item.description: Companies have been getting more out of employees for several years. Tight labor markets, digitization and remote work are among the reasons.
item.dc:creator: Talmon Joseph Smith
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Will ‘Take Over’ Strait of Hormuz, and How Russia Made Japan a Den of Spies
item.description: Plus, scientists find sugar in outer space.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, David E. Sanger and Adam Goldman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Strait of Hormuz mural in Tehran in April. President Trump has announced that he will impose tolls on shipping in the strait.

item.title: Oil Rises Above $85 a Barrel as Gulf Clash Escalates
item.description: Brent crude, the international benchmark, breached $85 per barrel for the first time in a month.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian, Katie Robertson and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters

item.title: Trump’s Plan to Charge a Toll in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
item.description: President Trump announced a 20 percent fee on cargo through the waterway, despite his own administration’s position that such fees violate international law.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump described his plan to levy a toll as a way for the United States to recover the cost of providing military protection to vessels using the waterway.

item.title: Red State, Blue Governor: It Could Happen in Iowa. Would It Matter?
item.description: Democrats have real hope for candidates for governor in red states like Iowa and Ohio, but if Republicans seize supermajorities in the legislatures, their power would be limited.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen and Jamie Kelter Davis
item.media:description: Democrats want to block Republicans from winning a supermajority in the Iowa legislature in November, but the key will be winning over rural voters.

item.title: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
item.description: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold and Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: A lowered flag at the U.S. Capitol on Monday in honor of Senator Lindsey Graham, who died over the weekend.

item.title: Can Democrats Win Rural Voters Turning Away From Trump? It’s a Tough Sell.
item.description: House candidates like Beth Macy, author of “Dopesick,” have crafted messages based on lifetimes in rural regions. But messages need an audience, and many minds are closed to the party.
item.dc:creator: Kate Zernike
item.media:credit: Matt Eich for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I think there’s a lot of rage, and feeling like people in power don’t care,” said the best-selling author Beth Macy, who is running for Congress as a Democrat.

item.title: At 91, He’s Hiking the Appalachian Trail. Again.
item.description: Dale “Grey Beard” Sanders once set the record as the oldest person to complete the roughly 2,190-mile trail. Now, he’s back for his title.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cohen and Luke Piotrowski

item.title: ‘I Am Frustrated’: Emails Show Blanche’s Role in Trump’s Retribution Campaign
item.description: The cooperation of Todd Blanche will be a flashpoint in his confirmation hearing to be attorney general on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.category: Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: Attorney General Todd Blanche’s confirmation hearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee is scheduled for Wednesday.

item.title: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
item.description: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: Witnesses in Biddeford, Maine, heard gunfire early Monday morning.

item.title: ICE Shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Through Open Passenger Window in Houston, Witnesses Say
item.description: A congresswoman and a lawyer relayed witness accounts that diverge from Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s version of the fatal encounter.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín, Robin Stein and Devon Lum
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: Family members embracing next to a makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo at the location where he was shot by an ICE agent in Houston.

item.title: Ukraine Wants to Make Its Own Antimissile System, Not Just Patriots
item.description: The Ukrainian firm Fire Point has produced weapons that hit Russian territory. Its next goal, developing an antimissile system, is a much bigger challenge, experts said.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Missiles manufactured by the Ukrainian defense firm Fire Point and video footage of a burning Russian refinery at the company’s exhibition stand at a defense industry conference in Kyiv last month.

item.title: Europe, Flexing Muscle in Ukraine, Is Still on Sidelines in Iran
item.description: In a show of collective strength, President Emmanuel Macron of France hosted European allies at the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris. Yet Europe is still a hostage to America’s foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: Vehicles of the French Army’s Ninth Marine Infantry Brigade on the Champs-Élysées during the annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday.

item.title: How a Promising Navy Drone Boat Program Got Mired in Politics
item.description: Navy acquisition reforms upended a $2.1 billion contract competition. When it was over, several companies with ties to the Trump administration had made the cut.
item.dc:creator: Kate Kelly and Julie Tate
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Colin Demarest/Axios
item.media:description: The Corsair, an unmanned vessel made by Saronic Technologies, demonstrated at a defense industry event last year. Saronic, which has twice made the Navy’s list of potential contract winners, has forged strong connections to allies of President Trump.

item.title: China Purges Top Official Over Corruption and Sex Charges
item.description: Ma Xingrui is the third member of the Politburo to be purged since 2022. He once led the Xinjiang region and was previously head of China’s space program.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.category: Central Commission for Discipline Inspection (Communist Party of China)
item.media:credit: Vincent Thian/Associated Press
item.media:description: Ma Xingrui at a meeting in Beijing last year. He had been the Communist Party leader of the far western region of Xinjiang before his downfall.

item.title: The Strange Phenomenon of ‘Terminal Lucidity’
item.description: As they near death, some dementia patients recover mental faculties assumed to be long lost. Researchers want to know why.
item.dc:creator: Billy Brennan

item.title: Agitation in Dementia Can Be Helped by Medical Cannabis, Study Suggests
item.description: A combination of THC and CBD eased symptoms in an especially frail population: patients with advanced dementia near the end of their lives.
item.dc:creator: Pam Belluck
item.media:credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emilio Gonzalez, left, participated in a clinical trial of a cannabinoid treatment for agitation in people with advanced dementia. His son, Dennys Gonzalez, right, said it eased his father’s distress and frustration.

item.title: New York to Enact Nation’s First Statewide Moratorium on Data Centers
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul will sign an executive order pausing the construction of the largest data centers for one year while the state assesses their effects on the environment and energy use.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul of New York has backed artificial intelligence as a tool for research and good governance, but said innovation must be balanced with public benefit.

item.title: N.Y.C. Officials Inspecting Construction Sites After Building Buckles
item.description: The inspections were prompted by an incident in Midtown last week, an official said. It was unclear how many sites were being looked at.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri, Stefanos Chen and Patrick McGeehan
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: New York City has begun inspecting construction sites tied to firms who worked on the Midtown building that was dangerously unstable last week.

item.title: 75 Years Later, ‘The Catcher in the Rye’ Remains a Great American Novel
item.description: Sure, “Catcher in the Rye” has its detractors. But the influence of its wised-up yet winningly innocent hero keeps it among the Great American Novels.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Jacobs
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times
item.media:description: J.D. Salinger’s novel has sold some 80 million copies globally. The original cover features an illustration of a carousel horse.

item.title: The Democratic Establishment Had This Coming
item.description: The Democratic Party needs shaking up.
item.dc:creator: Mara Gay
item.media:credit: Will Matsuda for The New York Times

item.title: The Housing Crisis Is Also a Retirement Crisis
item.description: A home has stopped being just a place to live — it has also become an asset.
item.dc:creator: Kyla Scanlon

item.title: Jon Stewart Mocks Mitch McConnell’s Recovery Photo
item.description: The “Daily Show” host called the image “totally believable evidence that Mitch McConnell is not only alive, but the happiest boy in the hospital.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: In the Public Interest
item.description: We look at the Trump administration’s attempts to pressure news organizations.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: Dark Sky Defenders Raise Alarm Along the Border of West Texas
item.description: Residents fear that the Trump administration’s plans to build a border wall will threaten the region’s spectacular starry skies.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller and Emily Elconin
item.media:credit: Emily Elconin for The New York Times
